title: Immediate Visual Feedback When AI or Long-Running Operation Is Initiated
description: The system shall provide immediate visual feedback (< 200ms) when an AI or long-running operation is initiated. Buttons shall transition to a loading state and duplicate submissions shall be disabled immediately.
feedbackPattern: User clicks [Analyze Benefits Guide] → button immediately shows [⟳ Analyzing...] → display 'AI is analyzing your document...' — even before backend returns first status update

title: Skeleton Loading States for Structured Content Being Retrieved or Generated
description: The system shall display skeleton loading states for structured content that is being retrieved or generated. Use skeletons for: requirements list, plan details, employee details, dashboard cards, impact analysis results. Never show a blank page with only a spinner.

title: Long-Running AI Operations Display Meaningful Processing Stages
description: Long-running AI operations shall display meaningful processing stages. Stages must reflect actual processing steps, not invented percentages.

progressModalExample.title: AI Requirements Analysis
progressModalExample.footer: You can continue working in other areas.

title: No Fabricated Percentage Progress When Actual Progress Cannot Be Determined
description: The system shall not display fabricated percentage progress when actual progress cannot be determined. Use 'Step 4 of 7' stage counting instead of an invented percentage.

title: AI Conversational Responses Support Progressive Streaming
description: AI conversational responses shall support progressive streaming where technically feasible. Use SSE for AI chat, AI interview, requirement explanation, conflict explanation, and impact analysis narrative. Do not stream structured JSON or UI components.

title: Extended-Processing Message for Operations Exceeding Expected Duration
description: AI operations exceeding the expected response duration shall display an extended-processing message reassuring the user that processing is still active.

timeoutThresholds.message: This analysis is taking longer than expected. The AI is still processing your documents. You can continue waiting or leave this screen.

title: Retry and Recovery Options on AI Timeout
description: AI operations exceeding the configured timeout shall provide retry and recovery options. Users shall be able to try again, view partial results, or return to requirements without losing uploaded documents.

title: Preserve Successful Results If Multi-Stage AI Operation Partially Fails
description: The system shall preserve successfully completed results if a multi-document or multi-stage AI operation partially fails. Do not discard successful results.

title: Long-Running AI Operations Support Background Processing
description: Long-running AI operations shall support background processing where appropriate, allowing users to navigate away without cancelling the operation.
globalHeaderIndicator: 🔄 1 AI Job — click to see AI Job Center panel

title: Notify Users When Background AI Operation Completes
description: The system shall notify users when a background AI operation completes. The notification shall include a summary of results and a direct action to review them.

title: Consistent AI Job Status Model Across the Application
description: The system shall provide consistent AI job statuses including Queued, Processing, Completed, Failed, Cancelled, Partial, Timed Out, and Retrying across all AI operations.

title: Transient AI Failures Trigger Automatic Retry with Exponential Backoff
description: Transient AI service failures and rate-limit errors shall trigger automatic retry with exponential backoff. See REQ-AI-RELIABILITY-002 for retry configuration.

title: Business-Friendly Error Messages — No Raw HTTP Errors Exposed to Users
description: The UI shall present business-friendly error messages rather than raw API or HTTP errors. HTTP 429 shall never be the primary user-facing error message.

title: Retry Failed AI Operation Without Re-Uploading Document
description: Users shall be able to retry a failed AI operation without re-uploading the original document. The backend shall retain Document ID, Job ID, and Request ID to support retry of an existing operation.

title: Accessible Status Announcements for AI Processing and Completion Events
description: The system shall provide accessible status announcements for AI processing and completion events using aria-live regions. Loading indicators must not rely solely on animation. Example: 'Document analysis is 3 of 7 stages complete.'
ariaExamples: Document analysis is 3 of 7 stages complete.
AI processing failed. Your document has been saved.
